title: polyalthia subcordata
intro: Polyalthia subcordata, also known as the Padauk tree or the Khasi tree, is a species of evergreen tree native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. The bark and roots of this tree have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Polyalthia subcordata include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The bark is often used to make herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
